sutta: monks possessed of 5 qualities among cannot enter and abide in right concentration what 5 here in monks a monk cannot endure sights sounds smell taste and touches monks possessed of these 5 qualities among cannot enter and abide in right concentration that's the end of the suta
commentary:
here this is saying that if a man cannot endure sight sound smells taste and touches then he cannot compose his mind why because these side sounds etc can distract him either distract him he's so distracted he cannot compose his mind or they discomfort him so much that he can't bear the discomfort and he's so agitated by it that he cannot compose his mind the first jana for a person to enter the first jana in the definition of the first jana the buddha said not secluded from sen's desires secluded from unwholesome conditions among enters and abides in the first jhana so a person has to be able to uh become a loof from the world of the 5 senses inside sound smell stays and touches before he can enter the vagina
